title: MaleCNS GABAergic Medulla
prompts: What GABAergic neurons in the medulla are available from MaleCNS Dataset?

messages.content: What GABAergic neurons in the medulla are available from MaleCNS Dataset?

Here’s the best partial answer I can give from the outputs you shared. This is incomplete because we hit the tool-round limit, so I’m sticking to what’s clearly present in the available data.

What I can confirm (from the medulla-related entries with GABAergic identity, not necessarily labeled as MaleCNS in the dataset)
- medulla tangential neuron vGATMtnew (FBbt_00110980)
  - Identity: GABAergic neuron in the medulla
  - Description: Medulla tangential neuron with extensive branching covering layers M7–M10; explicitly described as GABAergic
  - Synaptic/anatomy note: part of the medulla tangential class that innervates a broad portion of the medulla
  - Synonym: Mtnew1
  - Relevance: A clearly identified GABAergic medulla neuron, but not shown as a MaleCNS dataset entry in the provided results

- transmedullary neuron vGATTmnew (FBbt_00110981)
  - Identity: GABAergic transmedullary neuron
  - Description: labeled as a medulla transmedullary neuron with GABAergic identity
  - Relevance: another explicit GABAergic medulla neuron noted in the medulla-related lists, not clearly tied to MaleCNS in the provided data

- transmedullary neuron Tm3 (Tm3) in medulla (from NeuronsPostsynapticHere results)
  - Identity: Tm3 (FBbt_00003791 appears in the PostsynapticHere listing)
  - Description: GABAergic transmedullary neuron in the medulla
  - Relevance: confirms a GABAergic medulla neuron in the broader dataset; again, not clearly labeled as MaleCNS in the outputs I have

What is uncertain or not clearly shown in the provided MaleCNS-specific outputs
- I did not find explicit medulla GABAergic neurons that are clearly labeled as part of the MaleCNS Dataset in the results you shared. The above three entries are GABAergic medulla neurons, but their assignment to the MaleCNS dataset is not evident in the outputs I can access right now.

Two or three quick clarifying questions to narrow this for a follow-up (so I can pull a focused, lower-tool set)
- Do you want me to search specifically for neurons that are explicitly labeled as part of the MaleCNS Dataset (i.e., entries whose metadata or synonyms show a MaleCNS:… tag or dataset association), rather than any GABAergic neuron with medulla involvement?
- Should I restrict to neurons with soma in the medulla (as opposed to neurons with terminals in the medulla but soma elsewhere), or include both medulla-soma and medulla-innervation cases?
- How many candidate neurons would you like me to report (e.g., top 5, top 10), given the current data gaps for MaleCNS-specific GABAergic medulla neurons?

If you’d like, I can proceed with a tighter, MaleCNS-focused query plan once you answer those, aiming to identify any medulla GABAergic neurons that are explicitly tagged as MaleCNS when available.
